lessons.definition: Favorable or advantageous; resulting in good
examples: The new policy was beneficial to everyone.

lessons.definition: The maximum amount something can hold or contain
examples: The stadium has a capacity of 50,000.
She has a great capacity for learning.

lessons.definition: A task or situation that tests someone's abilities
examples: This project is a real challenge.

lessons.definition: To work jointly on an activity or project
To focus all one's attention on a particular object
examples: I need to concentrate on my work.
He concentrated all his efforts on passing.

lessons.definition: Feeling or showing certainty about something
examples: She is confident about her abilities.

lessons.definition: A result or effect of an action or condition
Notably large in size, amount, or extent
examples: A considerable amount of time was needed.

lessons.definition: Acting or done in the same way over time
To give help or assistance towards achieving something
examples: Everyone can contribute to the solution.
He contributed greatly to the team.

lessons.definition: To cause someone to believe firmly in something
examples: I'll try to convince him to join us.
She convinced me to try again.

lessons.definition: Of great importance, especially in the success or failure
To clearly show the existence or truth of something
examples: Let me demonstrate how it works.

lessons.definition: To cause something to occur in a particular way
examples: We need to determine the cause.
